status.next_allowed_action: Run the baseline smoke check, then implement the SpatialIndex facade using d3-quadtree for task 1.3.

decision_log.reason: The work spans graph-engine architecture, workbench integration, offline HTML, browser regression scripts, SpatialIndex, community wash behavior, and old renderer cleanup.

decision_log.decision: Create a child implementation branch only when execution starts
decision_log.reason: The current step is document planning. Code implementation needs a dedicated branch from the current architecture branch so the previous work remains reviewable.
decision_log.source: user direction and AGENTS.md branch rule

decision_log.decision: Keep DOM and SVG for this architecture pass
decision_log.reason: The known failures come from interaction ownership and browser default behavior, not proven rendering throughput limits.

decision_log.decision: Reuse existing Node tests and browser regression scripts by default
decision_log.reason: The repo already has graph-engine tests and Playwright-driven regression scripts through npx; adding a new dependency is not required for the plan.

decision_log.decision: SpatialIndex must become the actual graph hit-testing source
decision_log.reason: The user explicitly wanted this recorded so it is not forgotten, and the design rejects interface-only completion.

decision_log.decision: Keep static-renderer.ts only as a thin compatibility shell if it remains
decision_log.reason: The desired cleanup is deleting old renderer responsibilities, not forcing a risky filename deletion when a narrow shell preserves public API compatibility.
decision_log.source: design review and implementation surface inspection

decision_log.decision: Graph-owned wheel, trackpad wheel, and pinch-like ctrl/meta wheel must zoom the graph, not the browser page
decision_log.reason: The graph must own interaction on nodes, edges, community washes, and blank canvas. Text editing surfaces remain blockers.

decision_log.decision: Use d3-quadtree as the SpatialIndex node-search core
decision_log.reason: A mature D3 spatial-search primitive is already present through d3-force's dependency tree and is a better foundation than hand-rolling a uniform grid.

decision_log.decision: Make graph world bounds layout-driven instead of treating 1000x680 as a hard interaction cage
decision_log.reason: Pinned and dragged outlier nodes must not be silently clamped back by the old render model or viewport world size.

decision_log.decision: Execute the interaction vertical chain before extracting GraphFacade
decision_log.reason: User chose plan-eng-review D3 option A so the first real ownership transfer is Browser event -> GraphGestures -> SpatialIndex -> GraphViewport/GraphState -> GraphRenderer, avoiding facade-only progress.

phases.goal: Open the implementation branch and record the full interaction risk audit before changing behavior.

acceptance.check: Current branch is codex/graph-six-layer-architecture with no unrelated worktree changes.
evidence: git status --short --branch -> ## codex/graph-six-layer-architecture

evidence: npm run test --workspace=@llm-wiki/graph-engine -> 198 tests passed, 0 failed

acceptance.check: The interaction risk audit names expected behavior, owner layer, verification method, and target phase for every listed risk.
evidence: docs/graph/2026-06-16-interaction-risk-audit.md covers trackpad zoom, browser zoom, native selection, fast release, pointer cancel, toolbar/search/drawer/minimap boundaries, hover anchor, data refresh, keyboard, and touch.
rg coverage check found all required risk labels in docs/graph/2026-06-16-interaction-risk-audit.md

acceptance.check: A clean-start progress commit is recorded before implementation code changes.
evidence: f827f01 docs: record graph six-layer clean start

phases.goal: Establish the real interaction vertical chain foundation and SpatialIndex before facade extraction.

Graph-owned target and blocker contracts are covered without relying on DOM stacking order.
evidence: GRAPH_OWNED_TARGET_KINDS and GRAPH_GESTURE_BLOCKER_TARGET_KINDS define graph-owned vs blocker targets.
isGraphOwnedGestureTarget, isGraphGestureBlockerTarget, and graphGestureTargetOwnership expose the ownership contract.
gestures.test.ts covers the stable graph-owned/blocker contract without DOM stacking order.

tasks.title: Define graph-owned target and blocker contracts
Implement SpatialIndex facade using d3-quadtree for node search
Add interaction contract tests independent of DOM stacking order

phases.goal: Make GraphGestures the sole raw input and browser-default policy owner.

acceptance.check: Graph-owned wheel, trackpad-like wheel, pointer, and graph-focused keyboard behavior is routed through GraphGestures.
Graph-owned surfaces do not trigger browser page zoom, native text selection, or native drag selection.
Fast node drag release persists the final released position.
Workbench and offline browser regressions exit 0 with page zoom metrics recorded.

tasks.title: Move root wheel and pointer event ownership into GraphGestures controller
Implement graph-owned browser default behavior policy
Commit fast-release drag using final intent coordinates
Limit keyboard ownership to graph focus and active gestures
Cover cancellation and blocker boundaries in tests and browser scripts

phases.goal: Close coordinate and runtime state loops through GraphViewport and GraphState.

acceptance.check: No old renderer-local coordinate formulas drive graph behavior.
Hover, selection, focus, active drag, pins, and viewport have one GraphState-owned path.
Layout bounds, minimap, fit, SpatialIndex, and hover anchors support nodes outside the old 1000x680 world.
Workbench and offline browser regressions exit 0, including old pin compatibility.

tasks.title: Remove renderer-local coordinate calculations that bypass GraphViewport
Drive hover, selection, focus, drag, pins, and viewport from GraphState snapshots
Make world size layout-bounds-driven instead of a hard 1000x680 interaction cage
Preserve old workbench and offline pin data under new bounds
Handle graph data refresh while dragging without stale state
Verify projected overlays after zoom, pan, drag, and drawer resize

phases.goal: Create GraphFacade after the real interaction chain is stable while preserving public API compatibility.

acceptance.check: createGraphEngine remains compatible for workbench and offline HTML callers.
GraphFacade is the only layer that knows both graph internals and host callbacks.
Workbench and offline HTML use the same facade capability contract.
npm run test, typecheck, and build for @llm-wiki/graph-engine exit 0, and @llm-wiki-agent/web typecheck exits 0.

tasks.title: Extract GraphFacade around the current public graph engine API
Declare the six owner layers in code exports and tests
Define host and offline facade capability contracts
Add facade and renderer boundary tests

phases.goal: Split GraphRenderer into focused drawing modules and slim static-renderer.ts into a compatibility shell.

acceptance.check: Renderer modules receive snapshots or render models and draw only.
static-renderer.ts no longer binds root graph wheel, pointer, or dblclick listeners.
Renderer boundary tests prevent renderer modules from owning gesture meaning or host callbacks using runtime contracts.
graph-engine test, typecheck, and build exit 0.

tasks.title: Extract node, edge, community wash, minimap, and overlay drawing modules
Extract toolbar, search, legend, and offline reader drawing modules
Reduce static-renderer.ts to composition and compatibility only
Add renderer boundary checks

acceptance.check: Dragged or pinned outliers influence wash shape only within caps.
Nodes can be dragged outside community washes without changing membership.
Node hits outrank community wash hits, and community hits outrank blank hits.
Community wash caps do not grow unbounded when layout bounds expand.
Community wash, workbench, and offline browser regressions exit 0.

tasks.title: Lock community wash cap fixtures against expanded layout bounds
Apply bounded outlier influence without membership mutation
Use SpatialIndex priority for node, community, and blank hits
Verify wash behavior in workbench and offline browser paths

phases.goal: Remove old interaction paths and complete dual-target final verification.

acceptance.check: Full verification command set exits 0.
static-renderer.ts no longer owns raw graph interaction, coordinate conversion, or graph hit testing.
Runtime contract tests prove old responsibilities did not move to a new renderer file under another name.
Workbench and offline browser evidence artifacts are recorded.
Progress file records completed overall status and residual risk.

tasks.title: Delete old renderer-owned interaction, coordinate, hit-test, and hidden-state paths

Run final verification command set
